root_text.sn56.39:1.1: “Ye hi keci, bhikkhave, samaṇā vā brāhmaṇā vā ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānanti …pe…
root_text.sn56.39:1.2: ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānanti, te aññassa samaṇassa vā brāhmaṇassa vā mukhaṁ ullokenti:
root_text.sn56.39:1.3: ‘ayaṁ nūna bhavaṁ jānaṁ jānāti, passaṁ passatī’ti.
root_text.sn56.39:2.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, tūlapicu vā kappāsapicu vā lahuko vātūpādāno same bhūmibhāge nikkhitto.
root_text.sn56.39:2.2: Tamenaṁ puratthimo vāto pacchimena saṁhareyya, pacchimo vāto puratthimena saṁhareyya, uttaro vāto dakkhiṇena saṁhareyya, dakkhiṇo vāto uttarena saṁhareyya.
root_text.sn56.39:2.5: Evameva kho, bhikkhave, ye hi keci samaṇā vā brāhmaṇā vā ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānanti …pe…
root_text.sn56.39:2.6: ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānanti, te aññassa samaṇassa vā brāhmaṇassa vā mukhaṁ ullokenti:
root_text.sn56.39:2.7: ‘ayaṁ nūna bhavaṁ jānaṁ jānāti, passaṁ passatī’ti.
root_text.sn56.39:3.1: Ye ca kho keci, bhikkhave, samaṇā vā brāhmaṇā vā ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānanti …pe…
root_text.sn56.39:3.2: ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānanti, te na aññassa samaṇassa vā brāhmaṇassa vā mukhaṁ ullokenti:
root_text.sn56.39:3.3: ‘ayaṁ nūna bhavaṁ jānaṁ jānāti, passaṁ passatī’ti.
root_text.sn56.39:4.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, ayokhīlo vā indakhīlo vā gambhīranemo sunikhāto acalo asampakampī.
root_text.sn56.39:4.2: Puratthimāya cepi disāya āgaccheyya bhusā vātavuṭṭhi, neva saṅkampeyya na sampakampeyya na sampacāleyya; pacchimāya cepi disāya …pe… uttarāya cepi disāya …pe… dakkhiṇāya cepi disāya āgaccheyya bhusā vātavuṭṭhi, neva saṅkampeyya na sampakampeyya na sampacāleyya.
root_text.sn56.39:4.5: Evameva kho, bhikkhave, ye ca kho keci samaṇā vā brāhmaṇā vā ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānanti …pe…
root_text.sn56.39:4.6: ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānanti, te na aññassa samaṇassa vā brāhmaṇassa vā mukhaṁ ullokenti:
root_text.sn56.39:4.7: ‘ayaṁ nūna bhavaṁ jānaṁ jānāti, passaṁ passatī’ti.
root_text.sn56.39:5.1: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yogo karaṇīyo …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yogo karaṇīyo”ti.

translation_text.sn56.39:1.1: “Mendicants, there are ascetics and brahmins who don’t truly understand about suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the path.
translation_text.sn56.39:1.2: They gaze up at the face of another ascetic or brahmin, thinking:
translation_text.sn56.39:1.3: ‘Surely this worthy one knows and sees.’
translation_text.sn56.39:2.1: Suppose there was a light tuft of cotton-wool or kapok which was taken up by the wind and landed on level ground.
translation_text.sn56.39:2.2: The east wind wafts it west; the west wind wafts it east; the north wind wafts it south; and the south wind wafts it north.
translation_text.sn56.39:2.4: It’s because the tuft of cotton-wool is so light.
translation_text.sn56.39:2.5: In the same way, there are ascetics and brahmins who don’t truly understand about suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the path.
translation_text.sn56.39:2.6: They gaze up at the face of another ascetic or brahmin, thinking:
translation_text.sn56.39:2.7: ‘Surely this worthy one knows and sees.’
translation_text.sn56.39:2.9: It’s because they haven’t seen the four noble truths.
translation_text.sn56.39:3.1: There are ascetics and brahmins who truly understand about suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the path.
translation_text.sn56.39:3.2: They don’t gaze up at the face of another ascetic or brahmin, thinking:
translation_text.sn56.39:3.3: ‘Surely this worthy one knows and sees.’
translation_text.sn56.39:4.1: Suppose there was an iron pillar or an Indra’s pillar with deep foundations, firmly embedded, imperturbable and unshakable.
translation_text.sn56.39:4.2: Even if violent storms were to blow up out of the east, the west, the north, and the south, they couldn’t make it shake or rock or tremble.
translation_text.sn56.39:4.4: It’s because that Indra’s pillar is firmly embedded, with deep foundations.
translation_text.sn56.39:4.5: In the same way, there are ascetics and brahmins who truly understand about suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the path.
translation_text.sn56.39:4.6: They don’t gaze up at the face of another ascetic or brahmin, thinking:
translation_text.sn56.39:4.7: ‘Surely this worthy one knows and sees.’
translation_text.sn56.39:4.9: It’s because they have clearly seen the four noble truths.
translation_text.sn56.39:4.11: The noble truths of suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the path.
translation_text.sn56.39:5.1: That’s why you should practice meditation …”

variant_text.sn56.39:4.2: neva saṅkampeyya → neva naṁ saṅkampeyya (bj, pts1ed)
